paragraphs.body: Bond of Twins Over time people have only considered twins to look identical or fraternal but what about the twins that do not fall into those stereotypes? What about twins that are of different genders or twins that do not even look like they should be a part of the same family? Has everyone just normalized twins looking identical or fraternal and cannot fathom there being a supposed third type? According to Better Health Channel, “It is proposed that the egg splits in two, and each half is then fertilized by a different sperm. This theory is an attempt to explain why some fraternal twins look identical.” In other words, scientists have discovered that there could be a third type of twins out there that does not fall into this fixed idea that there are only two types of twins and can only be two types.

annotations.text: According to Better Health Channel, “It is proposed that the egg splits in two, and each half is then fertilized by a different sperm. This theory is an attempt to explain why some fraternal twins look identical.” In other words, scientists have discovered that there could be a third type of twins out there that does not fall into this fixed idea that there are only two types of twins and can only be two types.

paragraphs.body: For years now I have been told that my twin and I are not fraternal twins but instead we are identical twins and for years now I have been fighting these accusations. For years, I have always wondered what must happen during the development of the two babies to come out looking alike but also being fraternal twins. After reading the Better Health Channel “Twin-identical and fraternal” article I finally have some closure but more questions. More questions about how this topic has not been researched and why people still only think that there are two types of twins. More questions about the true differences between having identical twins and fraternal twins and how that can impact someone's life every day. More questions about why people can adapt to there being more dog breeds but not that there are more types of twins. Better Health Channel notes that “identical twins have a high likelihood of experiencing the same illnesses or having the same health condition. Identical twins are also compatible for organ transplantation, should it be required.” Better Health Channel points out that it is important to know if the twins are identical or fraternal for health-related issues. Identical twins can be each other's organ donors. In contrast, although fraternal twins share similar genetic information, they cannot be each other's donor without doing extra tests. This brings up the question of why there is such a substantial difference between identical and fraternal twins genetically and physically.

annotations.text: I have been fighting these accusations.
annotations.comment: This sentence suggest a negative connotation. Why is the distinction a problem? Let's talk :)

annotations.text: why people still only think that there are two types of twins.
annotations.comment: I'm interested in these ideas, but I still don't have context! What is the third (or 4th? 5th?)  type? Why?

annotations.text: More questions about why people can adapt to there being more dog breeds but not that there are more types of twins.

annotations.comment: There are many different ideas in this paragraph. If you begin the essay with a more complete summary then I think you'll be able to better organize your ideas :)

paragraphs.body: My twin and I have always been told that we look too identical to be considered fraternal twins. Over the years, I have met multiple sets of twins; some have been identical, and some have been fraternal. Most of the time I can tell which sets are fraternal or identical based on their appearance. If they look exactly alike, they are identical twins. If they do not look alike or are of different genders, they are clearly fraternal twins. But sometimes it is harder due to they look alike but are fraternal like my twin and me. Nevertheless, the one thing that all the twins had in common was a bond that nobody else could ever experience in their life. A bond between twins is a bond that nobody else understands and twins can hardly explain it themselves. Having a twin is like having a built-in best friend who will understand you to another level that not a soul knows.

annotations.text: A bond between twins is a bond that nobody else understands and twins can hardly explain it themselves.
annotations.comment: Interesting. Let's discuss the impact of this bond!

annotations.text: My twin and I have always been told that we look too identical to be considered fraternal twins.
annotations.comment: Nice job revealing your credibility and connection in terms of this topic!

paragraphs.body: Going through life with my twin as my built-in best friend has been amazing and I would never change it for anything else in the world. It is the only thing I know, and I know that if I lose any of the incredible benefits that come with being a twin I would crumbly up. For me personally the bond that I share with Claire is something that I know I will cherish for the rest of my life. We have grown up with each other and learned so much together. We have gone through huge milestones together and have gone through tragedy together and have come out the other side of it stronger than we went in it.

annotations.text: We have gone through huge milestones together and have gone through tragedy together and have come out
annotations.comment: Specifically? Let's talk :)

annotations.text: Going through life with my twin as my built-in best friend has been amazing and I would never change it for anything else in the world. It is the only thing I know, and I know that if I lose any of the incredible benefits that come with being a twin I would crumbly up.

paragraphs.body: Some of my favorite memories with her are randomly going into each other's rooms and starting to sing some random song from the million genres of music we both love and adore. It is not just then when we do this. We do this when driving home from work, school, softball, or whatever other adventure we were just on, singing otherwise known as screaming our little hearts out. The songs usually consisted of some old Disney songs, Billy Joel, Mamma Mia, Kansas, The Beatles, Disney original movies songs, early 2000’s songs, and whatever other new artists we just become obsessed with. If you were ever to watch one of our softball practices Claire and I are having full-on performances with each other while everyone looks at us. We always find time to be goofballs and be chaotic little messes around anybody and bless everyone with our beautiful singing and screaming fits.

annotations.text: Billy Joel, Mamma Mia, Kansas, The Beatles,
annotations.comment: great concrete and specific details!

paragraphs.body: Additionally, I love it when we just talk about whatever new thing we are currently obsessed with because it changes quite frequently. I love finding new artists, television shows, and book series that I immediately show her so that she can become as obsessed as I have become. I love that we can just go to each other with whatever we just newly found and can be as obsessed and insane about the topic as we want. I will always adore and cherish the countless memories of learning every word from Hamilton and playing constantly in the car. When we finally watched it when it came out on Disney+, my father finally saw how all the songs interlocked with each other because he had been listening to it out of order for months. Another example is we both are obsessed with this television universe called Arrowverse which consists of multiple TV shows that revolve around the DC Comic superheroes. And we will just sit down and watch the shows and just enjoy being in each other's presence.

annotations.text: Another example is we both are obsessed with this television universe called Arrowverse which consists of multiple TV shows that revolve around the DC Comic superheroes.
annotations.comment: Again, great details, but how do all of these details and ideas work together to develop one central focusing idea with value and purpose? Just as importantly: who is your audience for this essay?

paragraphs.body: Ultimately I love just talking to her about anything and everything. All my questions about the randomness that comes into my brain at any point in the day. All the chaoticness that is in folded with those spontaneous inquiries that can come out of my mouth at any point. I love it when we drive home from work and talk about the weirdness that goes on working in retail at an outlet. Some of the weirdest experiences that we have witnessed are when somebody peed in the fitting rooms and did not tell us, and I walked into the fitting room and saw it. Or when someone just stole a stack of jeans and ran out the door with no care in the world about the consequences. We still do not know what he wanted with female jeggings that were too small for him. Furthermore, when we ended up closing both sides together and going crazy in the back of the store or working Black Friday and trying to convince this man to buy socks because they are “perfect stocking stuffers.” I love living life with her and experiencing all the bizarreness that comes with life.

annotations.text: All the chaoticness that is in folded with those spontaneous inquiries that can come out of my mouth at any point.
annotations.comment: It's interesting that you repeat the idea of chaos and spontaneity so often in this essay...
annotations.context_right: I love it when we d

annotations.text: We still do not know what he wanted with female jeggings that were too small for him.
annotations.comment: lol

paragraphs.body: To describe my bond with my twin is too hard to put into words. I think that every twin will describe their bond differently depending on how they were raised and what life events they went through with each other. For me, I would describe my bond with my twin as something special and something that I will cherish for the rest of my life no matter what. If we are hundreds of miles away, I will always know that I have somebody miles away from me who is cheering for me and if we are two seconds away. I know that there will be the same amount of support and encouragement as there has always been and will always be. I would also describe it as something magical and as something unpredictable. The bond will evolve as we evolve as people but that is something that I am looking forward to and cannot wait to continually cheer her on.

annotations.comment: another word related to chaos and spontaneity.

paragraphs.body: I feel as though the feeling of the bond is bigger than people can imagine. I feel that people who are twins are the luckiest. They have two soulmates in this world. A romantic soulmate and a platonic soulmate also known as their twin. Twins have that built-in soulmate from the very start of their life. They have their twins who they will go through life with and who will understand them like no one else. Even though some twins might want to deny that there is a bond and connection between each other and want to run away from it. In my opinion, they cannot run away from it because it is something that will stick with them forever and ever no matter how far away, they try to run. It will always catch up to them in the most unexpected way possible.

annotations.comment: another word related to chaos and spontaneity...
What do you mean?

paragraphs.body: Being a twin has taught me so much about life and how you should always cherish the life that you are given. After reading the article by Better Health Channel I was fascinated by the part about identical twins being able to be each other's organ donors. Being a fraternal twin, it makes me question that if my twin needed my help then why can’t I easily help her? We share at least fifty percent of the same genetic information so why can't I help her if she is in crisis? Why is it so much easier for identical twins to help each other? Is it just because they share a hundred percent of the same genetic information? Or is it because whenever anyone thinks about twins, they think of twins that look identical? Is being a fraternal twin that looks alike not stereotypical or follows the rules of being a twin even though we all share the same bond with our twin?

annotations.text: Being a twin has taught me so much about life and how you should always cherish the life that you are given.
annotations.comment: How? Why? Let's explore :)
